entries.title: Star And Stripe Is America Top Hero

context.label: Star and Stripe heads for Japan

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Star and Stripe is the top American hero and acts from personal loyalty to All Might’s legacy.
content.injection: Star should enter as a world-class pro with personal conviction, not as a generic foreign reinforcement.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia
sourceInfo.notes: Draft Saga Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

entries.title: Star Acts Before Full International Consensus

context.label: Star and Stripe heads for Japan

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Star moves to aid Japan before slower international politics can fully settle.
content.injection: Keep international response politically constrained; Star’s arrival is exceptional, not proof that every country is freely intervening.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia

entries.title: New Order Requires Touch And Naming

context.label: New Order rule limits become central

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: New Order can impose rules after Star touches a target and names it, making identity and wording central limits.
content.injection: Use New Order precisely: touch, naming, and rule limits matter in every use.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia
sourceInfo.notes: Draft Saga Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

ability.name: New Order
constraints: Requires touch, naming, and rule capacity.
antiLore: Do not make New Order unlimited or freely transferable.

entries.title: New Order Has Rule Count Limits

context.label: New Order rule limits become central

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Star cannot maintain unlimited New Order rules at once, so choices and tradeoffs matter.
content.injection: Do not let New Order solve every problem simultaneously; rule capacity and wording should constrain outcomes.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia
sourceInfo.notes: Draft Saga Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

constraints: Requires touch, naming, and rule capacity.
antiLore: Do not make New Order unlimited or freely transferable.

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The unstable fusion of Shigaraki and All For One complicates New Order’s ability to target him by name.
content.injection: During the fight, identity ambiguity should be a major defensive factor for Shigaraki/AFO.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia
sourceInfo.notes: Draft Saga Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: New Order is extraordinary but cannot trivially erase Shigaraki/AFO because of identity limits and combat pressure.
content.injection: Do not resolve the arc by having Star cleanly delete the main villain; her power meets specific counters.

entries.title: Shigaraki AFO Hybrid Is Not Complete

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Shigaraki/AFO is still stabilizing during the Star and Stripe fight.
content.injection: Keep the hybrid state incomplete; this matters to identity, body control, and AFO’s timeline.

context.label: Star sacrifices herself to damage Shigaraki/AFO

content.fact: Star uses New Order to damage the Quirk stockpile inside Shigaraki/AFO at the cost of her own life.
content.injection: Star’s final move should wound AFO’s plans and Quirks, not produce immediate victory.

entries.title: AFO Threat Is Global After Star Fight

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The Star fight confirms that Shigaraki/AFO is a global-level threat, not only Japan’s domestic crisis.
content.injection: Scenes after Star should recognize international stakes while keeping the main response centered in Japan.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia
sourceInfo.notes: Draft Saga Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

content.fact: Star’s damage delays Shigaraki/AFO enough to give Japanese heroes a narrow preparation window.
content.injection: Use the delay as a fragile planning opportunity, not as safety or resolution.

entries.title: New Order Should Not Be Given To Other Heroes

context.label: Star sacrifices herself to damage Shigaraki/AFO

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: New Order does not become a reusable hero resource after Star’s death.
content.injection: Do not transfer New Order to Deku, All Might, or another hero unless the user story explicitly creates an AU.
